header_summary:
Recent discussions in the Bitcoin community have highlighted the evolving use of sequence values in transactions, with an emphasis on the need for clearer signaling of transaction replaceability. Data from [Mainnet Observer](https://mainnet.observer/charts/transactions-signaling-explicit-rbf/) suggests a trend towards replaceability, with 75% of transactions over a recent 30-day period signaling this intent. Nonetheless, ambiguities remain regarding the specific usage of sequence values like MAX-1 versus MAX-2, prompting calls for enhanced analytical tools to better understand user preferences and network behaviors.

The dialogue on transitioning Bitcoin towards quantum-resistant cryptography, led by Pieter, underscores the complexities of disabling elliptic curve cryptography without disrupting current operations. Pieter advocates for a phased approach, favoring taproot-based constructions in the short to medium term to minimize economic friction. A broader strategy for migration, sensitive to technological and ecosystem readiness, is deemed crucial to mitigate risks associated with quantum advancements ([source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/76e281e7-c4b4-4267-9bda-edfd2ee19dc0n@googlegroups.com/T/#mc53fc112a101b6722577c03c0f4f1fe036c1504b)).

Sean's contributions focus on the procedural aspects of developing Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs), advocating for an initial focus on drafting before delving into the complexities of test vectors. This structured approach aligns with BIP 3 guidelines and ensures foundational clarity before addressing more detailed components of the proposals. The intention to keep the community updated through a Pull Request indicates a commitment to transparency and collaborative development in the open-source community ([source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/d44d4a53-4895-4f0a-8411-ca7627c2324b@msgilligan.com/T/#m59efb956e0c0b8296680074550e3e4ae9270c1d0)).

recent_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Removal of BIP 125 RBF signalling in wallet transactions
recent_posts.summary:
- Sequence values in Bitcoin impact transaction replaceability and network behavior.
- Recent data shows 75% of transactions signaled replaceability without detailing specific settings.
- A shift to MAX-2 for all transactions could streamline and clarify signaling.

recent_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
recent_posts.summary:
- Pieter discusses Bitcoin's shift to quantum-resistant cryptographic methods, focusing on P2MR and P2TRv2.
- The viability of disabling elliptic curve cryptography hinges on choosing an appropriate implementation time.
- A phased approach to quantum resistance is recommended for alignment with technology and ecosystem readiness.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Informational BIP on Low-R Signature Grinding
recent_posts.summary:
- Sean emphasizes focusing on the draft before complex test vectors in BIP development.
- A Pull Request will be sent to a fork of the BIPs repository for structured updates.
- This approach reflects a pragmatic methodology in cryptocurrency software development.

recent_posts.title: Encoding art into the satoshi value field, and the unfilterable floor
recent_posts.summary:
- Art is created by embedding images into Bitcoin transaction values, each dot a payment.
- An encoding formula embeds image data into transactions without altering blockchain functionality.
- This artistic method challenges traditional blockchain data filtering, integrating art with currency values.

recent_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
recent_posts.summary:
- The BoP2 cryptographic scheme lacks compatibility with SUF-CMA Post-Quantum signature schemes.
- Hybrid cryptographic systems offer benefits but are less appealing due to maintenance challenges.
- Future cryptographic methods may adopt hybrids initially to ensure flexibility and efficiency.

recent_posts.title: Quantum Attack Game Theory
recent_posts.summary:
- The exploration of blockchain dynamics explores complex probabilistic theories.
- It highlights challenges in formulating intricate mathematical equations.
- A coupling argument shows equivalent probability spaces in mining scenarios.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] P2P UTXO Set Sharing
active_posts.summary:
- The BIP introduces new tools and processes for sharing UTXO sets in Bitcoin.
- New methods aim to streamline node setup and reinforce network independence.
- Detailed specifications ensure secure, efficient data transfers among peers.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Testnet 5
active_posts.summary:
- A new draft for Testnet5 addresses issues from Testnet4 and tests BIP54.
- Stakeholders are invited to review and comment on the draft PR on GitHub.
- The proposal is also discussable on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a new Bitcoin rule targeting 64-byte transaction encodings.
- The rule invalidates blocks with specific preimages that mimic valid transactions.
- It features a C++ function to ensure compliance, advising miners on handling violations.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal aims to optimize public key recovery from EC signatures in BIP-360.
- It introduces a recoverable EC leaf in P2MR to reduce witness size without quantum risk.
- Changes in validation logic and nonce derivation process are suggested to enhance security.

active_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts.summary:
- A Bitcoin-native LLM aims to enhance script analysis and protocol interaction.
- It will use a bifurcated approach with both static and live data processing capabilities.
- The initiative focuses on aiding wallet developers and protocol researchers.

active_posts.title: Default signet reorg history — was the fork at height 287766 (depth 19) deliberate?
active_posts.summary: - The investigation reveals insights into Bitcoin's signet reorgs essential for developers.  - A significant fork at height 287766 extended 19 blocks, raising management questions.  - It's crucial to determine if such reorgs are expected or anomalies in signet tests.

today_in_history_posts.title: Why not archive the backend of Bitcoin blockchain?
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The author queries if lightning nodes monitor only post-funding transaction activities.
- They discuss the impracticality of indexing on pruned nodes, questioning its viability for lightning.
- Proposals include preserving blockchain sections on multiple computers globally to assist users with limited storage.
contributors: アルム　カールヨハン

today_in_history_posts.title: Rebalancing argument
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The academic questions the efficacy of self-cycle transactions for network rebalance.
- Innovative mechanisms like Revive are proposed, yet benefits of rebalancing remain unclear.
- Maintaining balance might reduce fees and shorten paths, though smart fees could suffice.

today_in_history_posts.title: Estimating Likelihood for Lightning Payments to be (in)feasible
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Rene Pickhardt develops a theory for understanding payment network dynamics.
- His Python notebook illustrates new models on GitHub, although initial drafts have inaccuracies.
- The work redefines success probabilities in the Lightning Network, dismissing old models.
